content: First-line pharmacotherapy: stimulants (methylphenidate or amphetamine formulations) with robust evidence for efficacy (~70-80% response rate). Long-acting formulations preferred for adherence and reduced abuse potential. Non-stimulants: atomoxetine (SNRI), guanfacine/clonidine (alpha-2 agonists), viloxazine—used when stimulants ineffective/not tolerated or as adjuncts. Behavioral interventions: parent training (children), classroom management, organizational skills training, CBT (adults). Combination therapy often optimal. Monitoring includes growth (children), cardiovascular parameters, sleep, appetite, mood changes. Treatment often continues long-term given chronic nature.
